predicted_questions.question_text: Hi, good morning. Naftali — you called out an increase in millennials and younger guests this quarter and that more than half of onboard revenue is now being booked before guests board (and you referenced ~70% pre-cruise penetration). Can you quantify how much the younger cohort has grown as a percentage of passengers year‑over‑year, and tell us whether that cohort is disproportionately driving the higher pre‑book penetration and APD? As a quick follow‑up, have you seen any shift in geographic sourcing (North America versus Europe/other) that would be helping explain the APD or pre‑book behavior?
predicted_questions.rationale: Draws on recurring_concerns.core_topics[1] (booking behavior by demographic) and reasoning_style.follow_up_pattern (multi‑part + follow‑up); references Naftali's comments that the quarter saw an increase in young guests and that >50% of onboard revenue is pre‑booked and ~70% penetration in pre‑cruise booking engines.

predicted_questions.question_text: Jason — demand for Royal Beach Club Santorini and Paradise Island sounds very strong. Can you walk us through whether these owned/branded destinations are driving ticket lift or simply shifting spend into pre‑cruise/onboard purchases? Specifically, for Santorini and Paradise Island, what proportion of the yield accretion to date is coming from higher ticket prices versus onboard/excursion (pre‑book) spend, and how should we think about that split as Cozumel, Perfect Day Mexico and Costa Maya ramp?
predicted_questions.rationale: Targets recurring_concerns.core_topics[2] (owned‑destination/product impact on yield and ticket lift) and references Jason's comments about Royal Beach Club Santorini, Paradise Island, and the upcoming Cozumel/Perfect Day Mexico/Costa Maya projects being accretive to yield growth.
